caption: The video appears to be a presentation or lecture, likely focused on systems thinking and its applications. The presenter, Gene Bellinger, is seen in a small window on the right side of the screen, wearing headphones and speaking. The main content of the video is a visual diagram displayed on a computer screen, which illustrates various interconnected concepts related to systems thinking. The diagram includes elements such as "awaken," "systems thinking," "models and methods," "developing models," "model sharing," "what people do," "buying & selling," "fight club," "leadership," "how people think," "we have a problem," and "future of society." These elements are connected by arrows, suggesting a flow or relationship between them. The presenter seems to be explaining the relationships and significance of these concepts within the context of systems thinking, possibly using the diagram as a visual aid to guide the audience through the material.
